mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.4 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observed cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during periods of tight monetary policy, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above the threshold, or the inflation signal shows zero predictive IC over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and corporate profit margins, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns, or the hypothesis fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio in a long-only backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal predicts negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below chance in out-of-sample testing.
